gaps: A snapshot field summarizing the linked opinion cluster's procedural posture would help distinguish historical merits records from petition-stage grants without fetching new case facts.
Base-rate tooling keyed by event kind and by sparse-historical-SCOTUS records would make the `other` versus `denied` label easier to calibrate.
helpful: fedcourts paths resolved the event path.
The local snapshot made the permitted target-case facts explicit.
fedcourts stats provided SCOTUS petition-disposition base rates.
fedcourts query surfaced the shape of rare granted SCOTUS priors.
notes: I used the provisioned snapshot for target-case facts and local corpus tooling only for aggregate priors and examples. The prediction is limited by the absence of docket entries, petition-stage dates, and visible cluster contents.
